key_points: Curved surface area (CSA) is the lateral area excluding the base(s).
Total surface area (TSA) includes all outer surfaces.
For hollow or composite solids, subtract inner parts or add all component parts.
Volume of solids = product of base area × height (for prism/cylinder) or special formula for cone, sphere, frustum.
Frustum of a cone → CSA = π(R + r)l, Volume = (1/3)πh(R² + r² + Rr).
formulas: Cone → CSA = πrl; TSA = πr(l+r); Volume = (1/3)πr²h
Hollow cylinder → Volume of material = πh(R²−r²)
examples: Cylinder r = 5 cm, h = 14 cm → TSA = 2π×5(14+5)=2π×5×19≈597 cm²; Volume = π×25×14≈1099.5 cm³
exam_tips: Always calculate slant height l before CSA of cone or frustum.
For hollow solids, subtract inner volume from outer volume to get material.
Check whether question asks for CSA, TSA, or volume.
For composite solids, divide into basic shapes, calculate SA/volume, and then sum.
